title: A Casserole for the Missing
theme: Some homes hold families. Some hold motive.

traits: replies to texts while processing betrayal
owns a structured coat for crisis scenes

beat_sheet.text: Cole Vale relocates to Bellweather after an emotionally negligent implosion and pretends work explains everything while quietly inheriting marina tackle room described as retreat supplies. Wine is present like a municipal policy failure. Someone mentions the basement as if it were a legal relative. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 1.00.

Noah Bennett hates the move and immediately distrusts Luke Hale, a hardware store owner with suspiciously healthy handyman energy and access to annex room described as retreat supplies. Someone mentions the basement as if it were a legal relative. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 1.07. A basement light switches on in an empty house.

A wine scene at The Candle Finch introduces the blood-adjacent tea towel, local gossip about the Christmas pageant incident, and the fact that everybody here has somewhere to hide from a conversation. Wine is present like a municipal policy failure. Someone mentions the basement as if it were a legal relative. Mara Bell hesitates before explaining old family office described as old family things. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 1.14.

Someone mentions the basement as if it were a legal relative. Evelyn Bennett hesitates before explaining old storm shelter from before the divorce. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 1.23. A basement light switches on in an empty house.

Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 1.72. The ceramic owl is back on the kitchen island.

Someone goes missing after mentioning the unopened letter, the old orchard chapel, and unused portable classroom holding archived disciplinary files in the same sentence. Wine is present like a municipal policy failure. Someone mentions the basement as if it were a legal relative. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 1.84.

The reveal ties ceramic owl to Blackthorn Vineyard, St. Agnes Regional, and a brunch resurrection event by routing through unused portable classroom holding archived disciplinary files instead of one simple basement. Wine is present like a municipal policy failure. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 1.97.

Noah Bennett spots the timeline error that every adult has spiritually rented around and asks why everybody in this county owns just storage. Luke Hale hesitates before explaining annex room described as retreat supplies. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 2.10. Someone who should be dead appears at brunch.

Wine is present like a municipal policy failure. Someone mentions the basement as if it were a legal relative. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 2.25.

Wyatt Bell confesses just enough to make the truth worse while the sheriff arrives exactly fourteen minutes too late and someone stares through the dusty window of annex room described as retreat supplies. Someone mentions the basement as if it were a legal relative. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 2.41. The sheriff sees fresh mud on the boat key.

Sequel bait lands when the ceramic owl reappears and someone whispers about the Winterson case, the vineyard fire, and another offsite room nobody has adequately explained. Wine is present like a municipal policy failure. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 2.58. A phone vibrates from inside the wall.

contradiction_ledger.entry: Luke Hale is called Daniel in one kitchen scene and Derek during the rain argument.
Cole Vale says Bellweather is in Vermont, then calls it outside Atlanta after two glasses of wine.
A dead best friend appears at brunch and nobody agrees whether this is denial, evil, or scheduling.
The basement belongs to a condo, a lake house, and an abandoned vineyard at different times in Bellweather.

dialogue_samples: "You think because you wear flannel and own a dock light I am supposed to trust you, Luke?"
"Mothers do not disappear, Noah. They evaporate into paperwork."
"I checked his social media likes, Cole. Nobody who enjoys motivational quotes is innocent."
"The blood-adjacent tea towel is not decorative anymore and you know it."
"Trust is just fear with a casserole dish, Wyatt."
"You seem upset," he said, making tea in a way that felt prosecutable.
"If that basement is innocent, why does it sound defensive?"
"I did not lie. I just told the truth in the order this town could survive."
fake_critic_blurb: "A Casserole for the Missing understands that menace is a regional utility." - County Suspense Quarterly
sequel_hook: Channel 8 Night Report hints that the vineyard fire had a witness who still hosts brunch.
synopsis: Inspired by true events in the emotional sense only, "A Casserole for the Missing" follows Cole Vale, an overextended professional woman who mistakes productivity for safety until unresolved hometown romance collides with Luke Hale, a comforting regional adult carrying portable suspicion and plausible access to offsite storage. As a child drawing stairs, Blackthorn County reveals once again that every secret comes home and every adult has somewhere emotionally alarming to stand in silence. Where every secret comes home.
